title: GDKVM | Reproduction Process
subtitle: GDKVM: Echocardiography Video Segmentation via Spatiotemporal Key-Value Memory with Gated Delta Rule
cleanP1: If a virtual environment is currently activated in your Shell (indicated by (base) or (env) on the left), please deactivate it first.
cleanNote: Note: uv's environment management features high isolation. In most scenarios, even without deactivating the Conda environment, directly creating a uv virtual environment will not cause dependency conflicts.
installUvDesc: This project uses uv for dependency management. Developed in Rust, it features highly efficient dependency resolution capabilities.
installUvUpgrade: Or update to the latest version:
troubleshootingAfterInstall: After successful installation, run uv sync again.
getCodeDesc: Clone the repository and specify the local directory name.
initDesc: uv will read configuration files, create a virtual environment, and install all dependencies.
initNote: For a newer environment, you can use the env_02 configuration. Refer to env/env_02/pyproject.toml.
activateNote: Note: Explicit activation is recommended to enable interactive debugging (e.g., Python REPL) and checking package status via pip.
activateVerify: Verify environment (should output the path inside project .venv):
dataDesc: We utilize the CAMUS and EchoNet-Dynamic datasets.
trainingConfigDesc: Select the appropriate train.sh script based on your Shell environment (zsh or bash), and configure the following environment variables to adapt to your hardware:
trainingHyperDesc: Edit config/config_gdkvm_01.yaml to adjust key hyperparameters:
outputsDesc: Artifacts (weights, visualizations) are saved to the directory specified in hydra.run.dir.
wandbDesc: Experiments use Weights & Biases (WandB) for offline logging.
wandbUploadDesc: After training, sync offline data to WandB cloud:
footerNote: All references to "today" or "now" in this guide refer to December 15, 2025.
